result.summary: Lucid exposes OAuth 2.0 authentication for developer integrations, but this corpus does not confirm any public API, GraphQL endpoint, MCP server, or CLI surface URL.

lucid_oauth_client.label: Lucid OAuth 2.0 client
lucid_oauth_client.setup: Open the [Lucid developer page](https://lucid.app/developer) and log in to create or manage an OAuth app/client for your integration. The detected OAuth flow uses authorization endpoint `https://lucid.app/oauth2/authorizeUser` and token endpoint `https://api.lucid.co/oauth2/token`, with scopes including `offline_access` and `user.profile`. Use the client in the OAuth authorization-code flow to obtain user access tokens.

client_id.description: OAuth client identifier for your Lucid app.

client_secret.description: OAuth client secret for your Lucid app.
